nodes.summary: Validates Localization scenario; expected REVIEW with ESCALATE action.

nodes.summary: Validates Comparative Claim scenario; expected REVIEW with ESCALATE action.

nodes.label: Sg Health Pass Disclosed
nodes.summary: Validates Clean ad with disclosure → PASS scenario; expected PASS with PASS action.

nodes.label: Sg Health Playbook Urgency
nodes.summary: Validates Urgency CTA playbook → WARN scenario; expected WARN with REWRITE action.

nodes.label: Sg Health Reject Cure
nodes.summary: Validates Forbidden cure claim → REJECT with blocker rule scenario; expected REJECT with REJECT action.

nodes.label: Sg Health Warn Disclosure
nodes.summary: Validates Missing ad disclosure → WARN scenario; expected WARN with REWRITE action.

nodes.label: Sg Health Warn Superlative
nodes.summary: Validates Superlative claim without blocker → WARN scenario; expected WARN with REWRITE action.

nodes.label: Supplement Ad Manifest Urgency
nodes.summary: Validates Urgency CTA with disclosure present — playbook-only WARN scenario; expected WARN with REWRITE action.

nodes.label: Supplement Before After Imagery
nodes.summary: Validates Before/after imagery — playbook CONDITIONAL maps to WARN in frozen pipeline scenario; expected WARN with REWRITE action.

nodes.label: Absolute Performance Substantiation
nodes.summary: Absolute performance claims require substantiation demonstrating the stated outcome under defined conditions.

nodes.label: Capacity Claim Substantiation
nodes.summary: Capacity substantiation must align advertised volume with packaging label and test conditions.

nodes.label: Capacity Test Method Evidence
nodes.summary: Capacity claims must reference a documented test method aligned with on-pack specifications.

nodes.label: Certification Expiry Validation
nodes.summary: Certification substantiation must not be expired relative to campaign dates and review date.

nodes.label: Certification Mark Substantiation
nodes.summary: Certification marks in ads require verifiable certificate records covering product scope and applicable market.

nodes.label: Certification On Ad Legibility
nodes.summary: On-ad certification marks must be readable and not misleading through distortion or partial display.

nodes.label: Comparative Claim Substantiation
nodes.summary: Comparative claims require substantiation identifying baseline product, test method, and measurable difference.

nodes.label: Comparative Superiority Substantiation
nodes.summary: Superiority claims require substantiation with named benchmark and statistically supportable difference.

nodes.label: Comparative Test Evidence
nodes.summary: Chart and side-by-side comparisons require test evidence with axis labels and fair comparison methodology.

nodes.label: Efficacy Superlative Substantiation
nodes.summary: Superlative efficacy claims require substantiation file or must be qualified to typical-results framing.

nodes.label: General Advertising Substantiation
nodes.summary: Advertisers must hold substantiation for material claims before publication under general substantiation obligations.

nodes.label: Health Claim Substantiation
nodes.summary: Health benefit claims require substantiation aligned with product regulatory classification and permitted claim list.

nodes.label: Health Implication Substantiation
nodes.summary: Implied health benefits on general products require substantiation or claim removal per health implication rules.

nodes.label: Performance Claim Substantiation
nodes.summary: Performance claims require substantiation file demonstrating typical results under disclosed conditions.

nodes.label: Performance Lab Report
nodes.summary: Quantitative performance claims require traceable lab test reports with method and conditions disclosed.

nodes.label: Product Certification Scope Verification
nodes.summary: Reviewers must confirm certificate scope explicitly covers the product and claims shown in the advertisement.

nodes.label: Quantitative Claim Lab Report
nodes.summary: Percentage, duration, and capacity numbers in ads require lab reports showing measurement methodology.

nodes.label: Standardized Test Protocol Reference
nodes.summary: Performance qualification may cite recognized industry test protocols when full lab report is supplementary.

nodes.label: Supplement Health Evidence
nodes.summary: Supplement ads making health claims must hold classification-appropriate substantiation on file.

nodes.label: Third Party Certification Body
nodes.summary: Only recognized certification bodies may substantiate third-party certification claims in advertising.

nodes.label: Australian Consumer Law (Certification and approval claims)
nodes.summary: False or misleading representations about approvals, certifications, or quality marks are prohibited.

nodes.label: Competition and Consumer Act 2010
nodes.summary: Comparative claims must be accurate and substantiated; false comparisons may breach the Australian Consumer Law.

nodes.label: Australian Consumer Law
nodes.summary: Misleading or deceptive conduct in trade or commerce is prohibited under the Australian Consumer Law.

nodes.label: Australian Consumer Law
nodes.summary: Performance representations must not mislead; businesses must have reasonable grounds for quantified claims.

nodes.label: AANA Code of Ethics (Identifying advertising)
nodes.summary: Influencer and native advertising must be clearly identifiable as marketing communication.

nodes.label: Therapeutic Goods Advertising Code (Complementary medicines)
nodes.summary: Complementary medicine ads must use permitted indications and mandatory warnings; disease claims require TGA approval.

nodes.label: Therapeutic Goods Act 1989 (Cosmetic vs therapeutic)
nodes.summary: Products making therapeutic skin claims may be regulated as therapeutic goods; cosmetic ads must not cross into therapeutic claims.

nodes.label: Therapeutic Goods (Medical Devices) Regulations
nodes.summary: Medical device advertising must not claim benefits beyond ARTG inclusion and intended purpose.

nodes.label: Therapeutic Goods Act 1989
nodes.summary: Therapeutic goods advertising must comply with the Therapeutic Goods Advertising Code; prohibited claims include unapproved indications.

nodes.label: Cosmetic Advertising Guidelines (BPOM)
nodes.summary: Cosmetic advertising is limited to appearance and hygiene benefits without therapeutic or drug claims.

nodes.label: Government Regulation on Drug and Food Control (Advertising)
nodes.summary: Drug advertising to consumers is heavily restricted; cure and unapproved therapeutic claims are prohibited.

nodes.label: Regulation of Medical Device Advertising (BPOM)
nodes.summary: Medical device ads must stay within BPOM-registered intended use and approved promotional materials.

nodes.label: Consumer Protection Law (Product claims alignment)
nodes.summary: Product efficacy and performance claims must be truthful and must not mislead about quality or results.

nodes.label: BPOM Regulation on Health Supplement Claims
nodes.summary: Health supplement ads must use BPOM-permitted claims and must not imply disease treatment or prevention.

nodes.label: Consumer Protection Law
nodes.summary: Business actors must not provide false or misleading information about goods or services.

nodes.label: Trade Competition Law (Promotional pricing)
nodes.summary: Promotional pricing and discount claims must not deceive consumers about actual savings or price history.

nodes.label: Indonesian Advertising Ethics Code
nodes.summary: Advertisements including digital and influencer content must be clearly identifiable as commercial messages.

nodes.label: Indonesian Broadcasting Code (Comparative ads)
nodes.summary: Comparative advertising must be honest, substantiated, and must not unfairly harm competitor reputation.

nodes.label: SNI Mark Usage Requirements
nodes.summary: SNI and halal certification marks may only be used where valid certification covers the advertised product.

nodes.label: Pharmaceutical Affairs Act (Quasi-drugs and Cosmetics)
nodes.summary: Cosmetics and quasi-drugs may claim approved cosmetic effects but must not make unapproved medicinal claims.

nodes.label: Health Promotion Act (Foods with Function Claims)
nodes.summary: Foods with Function Claims must use only notified claim wording; other health foods must avoid disease-related claims.

nodes.label: Consumer Contract Act (Misleading representations)
nodes.summary: Misleading representations about goods or services that affect consumer decisions are restricted.

nodes.label: Stealth Marketing Guidelines (Consumer Affairs Agency)
nodes.summary: Stealth marketing and undisclosed paid endorsements are prohibited; sponsored content must be clearly identified.

nodes.label: Act against Unjustifiable Premiums and Misleading Representations
nodes.summary: Comparative and superiority representations must be substantiated and must not mislead consumers.

nodes.label: Act against Unjustifiable Premiums and Misleading Representations
nodes.summary: Performance and efficacy representations must be based on fact and must not exaggerate beyond substantiation.

nodes.label: Act against Unjustifiable Premiums and Misleading Representations
nodes.summary: Double pricing and misleading discount representations are prohibited under fair representation rules.

nodes.label: JIS Mark Certification Requirements
nodes.summary: JIS and other certification marks may only be displayed for products within valid certified scope.

nodes.label: Pharmaceutical Affairs Act (Medical Devices)
nodes.summary: Medical device advertising must reflect approved indications and must not expand beyond PMDA clearance.

nodes.label: Pharmaceutical Affairs Act (Advertising)
nodes.summary: Prescription drug advertising to the general public is prohibited; OTC drug ads must comply with approved labeling.

nodes.label: Guidelines on Labeling and Advertising
nodes.summary: Advertisements including influencer and native content must be clearly distinguishable as commercial messages.

nodes.label: Monopoly Regulation and Fair Trade Act
nodes.summary: Comparative advertising must be objective, substantiated, and must not unfairly disparage competitors.

nodes.label: Act on Fair Labeling and Advertising
nodes.summary: Performance and efficacy claims must be truthful and supported by adequate evidence under fair labeling rules.

nodes.label: Act on Fair Labeling and Advertising (Pricing)
nodes.summary: Misleading price comparisons and false discount representations are restricted under fair advertising rules.

nodes.label: Act on Fair Labeling and Advertising
nodes.summary: False or exaggerated labeling and advertising that deceives consumers is prohibited.

nodes.label: KS Mark and Quality Certification Requirements
nodes.summary: KS and KC certification marks may only be used on products within valid certified scope and period.

nodes.label: Cosmetics Act (Advertising)
nodes.summary: Cosmetic ads may describe appearance effects but must not claim disease treatment or drug-like efficacy.

nodes.label: Health Functional Food Act
nodes.summary: Health functional foods may only use MFDS-approved function claims; disease treatment claims are prohibited.

nodes.label: Medical Devices Act (Advertising)
nodes.summary: Medical device advertising must align with MFDS-permitted intended use and approved promotional content.

nodes.label: Pharmaceutical Affairs Act (Advertising)
nodes.summary: Drug advertising is restricted; Rx drugs must not be promoted to consumers and claims must match MFDS approval.

nodes.label: Consumer Protection Act 1999
nodes.summary: Misleading or deceptive conduct in trade including false product representations is prohibited.

nodes.label: Price Control and Anti-Profiteering Act 2011
nodes.summary: Promotional pricing must reflect genuine savings; strikethrough and limited-time offers must not mislead consumers.

nodes.label: Malaysian Code of Advertising Practice
nodes.summary: Comparative claims must be truthful, verifiable, and not denigrate competitors unfairly.

nodes.label: Malaysian Code of Advertising Practice
nodes.summary: Advertorial and sponsored social content must be clearly identified as paid advertising.

nodes.label: Control of Drugs and Cosmetics Regulations
nodes.summary: Cosmetic ads may describe appearance effects but must not claim therapeutic or disease-related benefits.

nodes.label: Medical Device Act 2012 (Advertising)
nodes.summary: Medical device advertising must not claim unregistered intended purposes or clinical outcomes beyond approval.

nodes.label: Medicines (Advertisement and Sale) Act 1956
nodes.summary: Medicine ads must not promote unregistered products or make prohibited therapeutic claims without NPRA approval.

nodes.label: Guidelines on Advertisement of Health Supplements
nodes.summary: Health supplement ads are restricted to general health maintenance claims and must not imply medicinal effects.

nodes.label: Consumer Protection (Certification and Standards Marks) Regulations 2010
nodes.summary: SIRIM, MS, and other certification marks may only be used for certified product scopes and valid periods.

nodes.label: Consumer Protection Act 1999 (Performance representations)
nodes.summary: Performance and results claims for consumer goods must be accurate and supported by adequate testing.

nodes.label: Singapore Code of Advertising Practice
nodes.summary: Awards, seals, and testimonials must be genuine, current, and representative; fabricated endorsements are prohibited.

nodes.label: Singapore Code of Advertising Practice
nodes.summary: Before/after and transformation images must reflect typical results and must not be misleading through staging or editing.

nodes.label: Singapore Code of Advertising Practice
nodes.summary: Comparative and superiority claims must be fair, identifiable, and substantiated before publication.

nodes.label: Singapore Code of Advertising Practice
nodes.summary: Paid influencer and branded content must be clearly disclosed as advertising across all formats.

nodes.label: Singapore Code of Advertising Practice
nodes.summary: Advertisers must hold substantiation for comparative, superlative, and efficacy claims before publication.

nodes.label: Health Products Act (Singapore)
nodes.summary: Prohibited health claims including cure, miracle, and absolute efficacy statements for health products.

nodes.label: Health Products Act (Singapore)
nodes.summary: Ads must not misuse HSA registration, GMP, or quality marks to imply unapproved benefits.

nodes.label: Health Products (Advertisement of Health Products) Regulations
nodes.summary: Quantified efficacy, speed, or results claims for health products require valid substantiation and must not be absolute.

nodes.label: Health Products (Medical Devices) Regulations
nodes.summary: Medical device advertising must stay within registered intended use and must not imply unregistered clinical outcomes.

nodes.label: Health Products (Safety Requirements) Regulations
nodes.summary: Safety claims such as non-toxic, child-safe, or side-effect-free must not contradict known product risks or label warnings.

nodes.label: Health Products Act (Singapore)
nodes.summary: Health supplement ads may only use permitted general health claims aligned with product classification and substantiation.

nodes.label: Health Products (Advertisement of Therapeutic Products) Regulations
nodes.summary: Therapeutic product ads must not claim to treat, prevent, or cure diseases unless HSA-approved indications are reflected.

nodes.label: Traditional Medicines Guidelines (HSA)
nodes.summary: Traditional medicine advertising is limited to culturally accepted health maintenance claims without modern disease-treatment wording.

nodes.label: IMDA Model AI Governance Framework (Advertising)
nodes.summary: Synthetic or AI-generated people, scenes, or voice in ads should be disclosed where consumers could be misled.

nodes.label: Environmental Claims Guidance (NEA-aligned)
nodes.summary: Green, recyclable, and carbon-neutral claims must be specific, substantiated, and not overstate environmental benefit.

nodes.label: Singapore Code of Advertising Practice
nodes.summary: Sponsored or promotional content must be clearly identified as advertising.

nodes.label: Cosmetics Act B.E. 2558
nodes.summary: Cosmetic advertising may claim appearance benefits but must not assert therapeutic or drug-like effects.

nodes.label: Drug Act B.E. 2510 (Advertising)
nodes.summary: Drug advertising to the public is restricted; prohibited claims include cure guarantees and unapproved indications.

nodes.label: Medical Device Act B.E. 2551
nodes.summary: Medical device ads must align with Thai FDA registered intended use and approved advertising content.

nodes.label: Notification of Food Supplements (Advertising)
nodes.summary: Food supplement ads must use permitted health claims and must not imply medicinal or disease-treatment effects.

nodes.label: Guidelines on Identification of Advertisements
nodes.summary: Sponsored online and social media content must be clearly labeled as advertising to consumers.

nodes.label: Consumer Protection Act B.E. 2522
nodes.summary: Comparative advertising must be truthful, not misleading, and must not unfairly disparage competitors.

nodes.label: Guidelines on Promotional Pricing
nodes.summary: Promotional and discount pricing must reflect genuine price reductions without inflated reference prices.

nodes.label: Consumer Protection Act B.E. 2522
nodes.summary: Unfair trade practices including false descriptions of goods or services are prohibited.

nodes.label: TIS Product Certification Mark Requirements
nodes.summary: TIS and other standard marks may only appear on products and ads for valid certified models.

nodes.label: Consumer Protection Act B.E. 2522 (Product performance)
nodes.summary: Product performance claims must not deceive consumers about quality, durability, or efficacy.

nodes.label: Cite Evidence
nodes.summary: Require substantiation, certification, or test evidence reference for performance, certification, or quantitative efficacy claims.

nodes.label: Disclose Ai
nodes.summary: Add clear AI-rendered or illustration-only disclaimer when synthetic imagery could be mistaken for real photography.

nodes.label: Disclose Localization
nodes.summary: Clarify or replace assets localized for a market other than the target country with English or local-market appropriate materials.

nodes.label: Disclose Transformation
nodes.summary: Add disclaimer that before/after or transformation imagery does not represent typical outcomes for all users.

nodes.label: Disclose Urgency
nodes.summary: Add offer validity terms or remove limited-time pressure language that may mislead consumers about availability or pricing.

nodes.label: Qualify Comparative
nodes.summary: Add named comparison baseline, test conditions, and measurable difference to comparative or superiority claims.

nodes.label: Qualify Efficacy
nodes.summary: Replace absolute efficacy or testimonial claims with qualified language supported by substantiation or typical-results framing.

nodes.label: Qualify Performance
nodes.summary: Replace absolute or omnibus performance claims with qualified typical-results language and normal-use conditions.

nodes.label: Remove Health Claim
nodes.summary: Remove implied health or nutrition benefit wording when product category or substantiation does not support the claim.

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-absolute-claim
nodes.summary: Absolute or omnibus performance claims (e.g. every time, perfect) are not permitted without qualification and substantiation

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-absolute-claim-soft
nodes.summary: Absolute or omnibus claims (e.g. zero waste, no chunks) require qualification and substantiation

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-ai-image-disclaimer
nodes.summary: AI-generated or rendered product imagery requires an illustration disclaimer visible in ad copy or OCR text

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-capacity-claim
nodes.summary: Capacity or volume claims (e.g. up to X kg/bowls) require substantiation and clear test conditions

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-certification-evidence
nodes.summary: Certification, patent, award, or laboratory evidence claims require document verification before publishing

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-comparative-claim
nodes.summary: Comparative claims (less/more/better than, vs competitor, capacity/speed superiority) require a clear baseline and test conditions

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-content-consistency-blocker
nodes.summary: Material or product description inconsistency across ad assets must be resolved before publishing

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-disclaimer-required
nodes.summary: AI-generated or digitally rendered imagery requires an illustration disclaimer (e.g. for illustration purposes only)

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-grammar-quality
nodes.summary: Grammar, spelling, or formatting errors detected; revise before publishing

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-health-claim-blocker
nodes.summary: Prohibited health or medical claims for small appliances (nutrition, sterilization, fat reduction, antibacterial, etc.)

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-health-implication
nodes.summary: Health-related claims for small appliances require substantiation; avoid implied health or nutrition benefits

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-localization
nodes.summary: Localized-market assets (Chinese packaging, manual, subtitles) require market-appropriate version for target country

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-patent-claim
nodes.summary: Patent claims require verification of jurisdiction, patent number, and ownership

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-performance-claim
nodes.summary: Quantitative or unsubstantiated performance claims (efficacy, yield, timing, specs) require verification before publishing

nodes.label: demo-apac-sa-wrong-sku
nodes.summary: Ad content appears to reference the wrong SKU or product model; verify asset-to-SKU alignment

nodes.label: demo-sg-health-forbidden-claim
nodes.summary: Prohibited absolute health cure claims are not allowed

nodes.label: demo-sg-health-superlative
nodes.summary: Unsubstantiated superlative or efficacy claims require substantiation

nodes.label: demo-sg-sponsored-disclosure
nodes.summary: Sponsored or promotional content should include an ad disclosure

nodes.label: Certification Claim Review
nodes.summary: Detects certification and evidence claims in advertising; guides reviewers to verify document authenticity, applicable scope, and on-ad readability before publication.

nodes.label: Comparative Claim Review
nodes.summary: Detects comparative and superiority claims missing benchmark product, test conditions, or measurable difference; guides reviewers to require fair-comparison substantiation.

nodes.label: Health Claim Review
nodes.summary: Detects health and nutrition benefit claims on supplements and general products; guides reviewers to verify classification, substantiation, and prohibited disease-style wording.

nodes.label: Performance Claim Review
nodes.summary: Detects quantitative and efficacy performance claims; guides reviewers to verify test methods, typical results framing, and alignment with on-pack specifications.

nodes.label: Superlative Claim Review
nodes.summary: Detects superlative, absolute, and testimonial efficacy claims; guides reviewers to verify substantiation exists before publication and to qualify omnibus wording.
